name: Saunders Farm
industry: Agri-tourism, seasonal attractions, festivals, farm camp, private events, and cider retail
location: 7893 Bleeks Road, Munster, Ottawa, ON K0A 3P0

decisionMaker.name: Mark Saunders
decisionMaker.role: Co-operator and family-business leader; took the reins with Angela Grant Saunders in 2010

observations.title: Two planning horizons appear on current pages
observations.detail: The homepage promotes current 2026 summer products, while the farm-information page still labels PumpkinFest, FrightFest, and A Country Christmas as 2025 with 2026 hours coming soon.
observations.implication: A coordinated announcement calendar and matching page updates can reduce seasonal confusion before ticket purchase.

observations.title: Several distinct audiences compete for attention
observations.detail: The current homepage presents Summer Maze Days, a new Corn Maze Festival, a one-night scare event, Farm Camp, corporate outings, celebrations, cider, food, and retail.
observations.implication: Separate recurring formats can let parents, horror fans, camp buyers, and corporate planners self-select quickly.

observations.title: Near-term content can connect directly to tickets
observations.detail: The official homepage includes date-specific purchase actions for active Summer Maze Days, the August 1 Corn Maze Festival, and the August 8 one-night scare event.
observations.implication: Each short can answer one objection, show one unique moment, and close on the correct event purchase action.

whyNow.title: Three summer campaigns are live or imminent
whyNow.detail: On 2026-07-20 the official site said Summer Maze Days was on now, the new Ottawa 200 Corn Maze Festival would begin August 1, and A Midsummer Night's Scream would run for one night on August 8.

scores.summary: Exceptional visual and seasonal inventory with an immediate ticket-conversion window; response estimate is tempered by the likelihood that an established attraction already has production partners.

concepts.title: Can You Solve Ottawa in 20 Seconds?
concepts.hook: Think you can solve Ottawa in 20 seconds?
outline: Open in first-person view at one branch of the Ottawa 200 corn maze.
Give the viewer a fast left-or-right choice.
Reveal one Ottawa history or agriculture detail represented in the maze.
Close with the August 1 start date and the official ticket action.
concepts.productionNotes: Use a stabilized chest-height POV plus one overhead or raised reveal. Do not disclose enough of the route to spoil the full maze.

concepts.title: One Scare Before Sundown
concepts.hook: This path looks harmless in daylight.
outline: Show the normal daytime path.
Reveal one lighting, sound, or performer setup without showing the final scare.
Let the performer explain the misdirection in one sentence.
Close with August 8 and the one-night-only message.
concepts.productionNotes: Film the setup in daylight and the payoff environment after dark. Keep the final effect unrevealed.

concepts.title: 50 Years in Five Farm Sounds
concepts.hook: You can hear 50 years of Saunders Farm in five sounds.
outline: Cut from hedge trimmer to wagon bell.
Move into a cider pour and crowd reaction.
Finish on one night-scare audio hit.
Have Mark or Angela connect the sounds to the current season.
concepts.productionNotes: Prioritize clean location audio and fast match cuts. Record the narration separately for intelligibility.

outreach.subject: Three short videos for the Ottawa 200 maze and August 8
outreach.opening: Hi Mark, I was looking through Saunders Farm's current summer launch pages.
outreach.body: You have three unusually filmable moments stacked together: Summer Maze Days on now, the Ottawa 200 Corn Maze Festival starting August 1, and the one-night Midsummer Night's Scream on August 8. I sketched a small conversion-focused series around those exact dates: a 20-second Ottawa maze challenge, a daylight-to-scare behind-the-scenes reveal, and a five-shot 50th-year farm story. Each would end on the specific ticket action rather than a general brand message. I'm with Que Media in Ottawa and we make entertaining and educational short-form video for local businesses.
outreach.callToAction: If useful, I can send the three shot outlines before asking for a meeting.

name: Automindz
industry: Automotive detailing, appearance protection, wraps, collision repair, and mechanical service
location: 1555 St. Laurent Boulevard, Unit B, Ottawa, ON K1G 1A1

observations.title: Basic official web presence for high-consideration services
observations.detail: The official site is a Google Sites page that presents services, a gallery, contact details, and a quote action without a deeper trust or case-study structure.
observations.implication: Process video and case proof can reduce uncertainty while a stronger conversion surface is developed.

observations.title: Broad service categories lack visible choice guidance
observations.detail: PPF, wraps, ceramic coating, paint correction, collision repair, and mechanical work appear together, without visible decision criteria, process duration, warranty detail, or distinct booking routes.
observations.implication: A recurring service-diagnosis series can educate a customer before the quote request.

observations.title: Only one official social account was verified
observations.detail: The official site names Instagram and does not surface a verified TikTok, YouTube, Facebook, or LinkedIn destination.
observations.implication: One vertical-video production system could create intentional multi-platform distribution rather than dependence on one discoverable account.

whyNow.title: July and August service promotion
whyNow.detail: The official site currently advertises 15% off most services during July and August. Exact exclusions and the final date are not published and must be confirmed before content production.

scores.summary: Excellent transformation and education inventory with a live offer and a clear conversion gap; the unverified decision-maker materially lowers contact confidence.

concepts.title: Polish, PPF, or Paint?
concepts.hook: Can this scratch be polished, protected, or does it need paint?
outline: Open on a real flaw under an inspection light.
Let a technician give a three-second diagnosis.
Demonstrate the deciding test.
Reveal the correct service and name the service that would waste the customer's money.
concepts.productionNotes: Use macro shots and cross-polarized or controlled inspection lighting. Obtain vehicle-owner permission and conceal licence plates.

concepts.title: Ottawa Winter Protection Stack
concepts.hook: PPF, ceramic coating, and rustproofing do not solve the same problem.
outline: Label each protection layer on one vehicle.
Show the surface or area each service protects.
Explain one Ottawa winter use case for each.
Close with a request for a service recommendation.
concepts.productionNotes: Use clean graphic overlays that identify paint surface, impact zones, and underbody without making unsupported durability claims.

concepts.title: 15% Summer Rescue
concepts.hook: What can one eligible summer service change on this vehicle?
outline: Document the intake flaw.
Show one decisive process step.
Reveal the finish in identical lighting.
State the approved promotion terms and quote action.
concepts.productionNotes: Automindz must approve the exact eligible service, exclusions, and expiry before any promotion language is published.

outreach.subject: A video idea that makes the July-August offer easier to choose
outreach.opening: Hi Automindz team, I found your July-August 15% promotion while reviewing the service mix on your site.
outreach.body: The offer covers visually strong work, but a new customer still has to work out whether they need paint correction, ceramic coating, PPF, a wrap, or body work before requesting a quote. I mapped a short series called 'Polish, PPF, or Paint?' using real inspection-light footage and a technician's quick verdict. It would turn the transformation into education and close on the summer offer only where the service is eligible. I'm with Que Media in Ottawa.
outreach.callToAction: If the idea is relevant, I can send a three-video shot plan built around the exact services you want to fill this summer.

name: ARTium Design Build Renovations
customerSegments: Homeowners seeking one accountable design-build team

observations.title: Homepage review proof sends visitors off-site
observations.detail: The live What Our Clients Are Saying section currently presents a Read all Reviews link to Google without rendering a client quotation in the fetched homepage content.
observations.implication: A short client-story format could place the problem, process, and outcome directly in ARTium's owned content.

observations.title: No short-form video destination is linked in the current footer
observations.detail: The official footer links Facebook, Instagram, Houzz, and Pinterest, but no TikTok or YouTube destination.
observations.implication: The company's detailed renovation education can be organized into a dedicated, searchable video series.

observations.title: Ownership story is detailed on About, not surfaced on the homepage
observations.detail: The current About page clearly explains Kaylie Chamberlin's 2025 takeover and identifies her as owner, while the homepage's main story emphasizes 25-plus years without surfacing her name.
observations.implication: A homepage-linked short-form owner story could carry that strong first-party narrative into discovery content while preserving founder equity.

scores.summary: High-value visual work and strong educational material meet off-site review proof and an ownership story concentrated on the About page; no verified 30-day business trigger was found.

concepts.title: The Torch Forward
concepts.hook: What stays the same when a 25-year renovation company changes hands?
outline: Open with Kaylie in one completed ARTium space.
Name one principle retained from Norm Lecuyer's era.
Show one process or client-experience improvement under current ownership.
Close on the continuity of the ARTium team.
concepts.productionNotes: The company must approve every ownership-transition statement. Use the public transition only as the basis for an interview, not as a complete script.

concepts.title: Reno Surprise Files
concepts.hook: This is the renovation surprise you want to find before demolition.
outline: Present one real hidden-condition scenario.
Show the pre-construction check that can reveal it.
Explain the budget or schedule consequence in plain language.
Close with one homeowner planning action.
concepts.productionNotes: Use approved project examples and conceal client addresses. Avoid definitive cost claims unless ARTium supplies them.

concepts.title: Nine Months in 30 Seconds
concepts.hook: Why does a major renovation need nine to twelve months of planning?
outline: Show as-built drawings and layout options.
Move through 3D rendering, budget review, permit, and selections.
Cut into construction and final warranty handoff.
Close with ARTium's published planning window.
concepts.productionNotes: Build a graphic timeline using only actual ARTium process stages.

outreach.subject: A useful ARTium video series for the next chapter
outreach.opening: Hi Kaylie and Lindsay, I was reviewing ARTium's renovation process and noticed two opportunities.
outreach.body: The site has strong teaching material, including the six-to-nine and nine-to-twelve-month planning windows, but that expertise is not organized into a visible short-form series. The homepage review section also sends visitors to Google rather than telling a client story directly on the page. I sketched 'Reno Surprise Files': one real renovation risk, one planning check, and one clear homeowner takeaway per video. A second piece could carry the Kaylie transition story already documented on your About page into short-form discovery content, connecting ARTium's next chapter to its 25-plus years. I'm with Que Media in Ottawa.
outreach.callToAction: I can send the three outlines first if you would like to assess the ideas without booking a call.

sources.label: Matching company LinkedIn
sources.publisher: ARTium Design Build + Just Basements on LinkedIn

name: Stonefields Estate
industry: Wedding venue, private-event estate, culinary events, and overnight event accommodation

observations.title: Verified channel set does not include TikTok or YouTube
observations.detail: The official site links Instagram, Facebook, and Pinterest, but no dedicated short-form or long-form video destination was verified.
observations.implication: Searchable venue education can extend beyond finished-event inspiration in a social feed.

observations.title: The offer has broadened beyond full weddings
observations.detail: The homepage remains wedding-led while also promoting petite weddings, private celebrations, galas, Dinner Club, and other experiences.
observations.implication: Separate short-form series can help each audience recognize the estate as relevant to its event type.

observations.title: Current feed themes can be extended with operational expertise
observations.detail: The live embedded captions include wedding-fashion trends, guest comfort, seasonal celebrations, property features, and Dinner Club.
observations.implication: A recurring coordinator, chef, or owner layer can connect polished visuals to the planning risk reduced by an all-inclusive service.

whyNow.title: Current expansion into private events, petite weddings, and Dinner Club
whyNow.detail: The site currently says 2026 private events and petite weddings are open for booking and embeds a caption that the Fall Dinner Club Series is live. The site does not expose a publication date, so this is current sales context rather than a proven 30-day announcement.

scores.summary: Premium visual inventory and a newly broader event mix create strong content fit; timing is useful but lacks a visible publication date.

concepts.title: Full Wedding or Petite?
concepts.hook: What changes when a full wedding becomes a petite wedding?
outline: Use split screen to show how the same estate supports two event sizes.
Have a coordinator explain one planning decision that changes at each scale.
Show the corresponding ceremony, dining, and guest-flow choices.
Close on booking a tour for the relevant format.
concepts.productionNotes: Film empty spaces and approved event footage from both formats. Do not imply fixed capacities or packages without current internal approval.

concepts.title: The Quiet Work Behind Guest Comfort
concepts.hook: Guests never see these three checks, but they feel all of them.
outline: Follow one staff member through the weather contingency.
Show ceremony-to-cocktail guest flow preparation.
Show one accommodation or overnight-readiness check.
Close on the benefit of the all-inclusive team.
concepts.productionNotes: Use real pre-event operations and avoid identifying a couple or event without consent.

concepts.title: Empty Barn to First Plate
concepts.hook: Here is what happens before Dinner Club's first plate reaches the room.
outline: Open on the empty event space.
Time-lapse the tablescape and lighting transition.
Move to the kitchen pass and first plate.
Have Stephanie or Steve explain why the public dining experience was added.
concepts.productionNotes: Capture the room from a locked camera position for a seamless transformation. Approve all event dates and ticket language before release.

outreach.subject: A short-form series for Stonefields' new 2026 audiences
outreach.opening: Hi Stephanie and Steve, I was looking at the current Stonefields site and noticed how much the offer has expanded.
outreach.body: Private events, petite weddings, and Dinner Club now sit alongside the all-inclusive wedding experience. I sketched a video series that helps those audiences see themselves at the estate. The first concept is 'Full Wedding or Petite?', using the same spaces to show how a full wedding and a petite wedding flow differently. A second follows the quiet guest-comfort work your team handles before anyone arrives, and a third takes Dinner Club from an empty barn to the first plate. I'm with Que Media in Ottawa.
outreach.callToAction: If useful, I can send the shot outlines before suggesting any meeting.

name: Lagois Design Build Renovate
industry: Boutique residential design-build renovation and custom homes
location: 5921 Prince of Wales Drive, North Gower, Ottawa, ON K0A 2T0

decisionMaker.name: Emma Kwan
decisionMaker.role: Interior Design Specialist and Director of Marketing

customerSegments: Homeowners planning for aging in place
Homeowners seeking a boutique integrated design-build team

observations.title: LinkedIn footer destination is misconfigured
observations.detail: The current homepage footer points to a LinkedIn administrative dashboard path instead of the public company page.
observations.implication: Correcting the destination is a small trust and discoverability win before promoting social proof.

observations.title: Distinct differentiators are text-heavy on the main conversion surface
observations.detail: Net-zero renovation, Adaptiv living in place, indoor-outdoor living, the three-phase process, and the five-year warranty are described primarily through text and still imagery on the inspected homepage.
observations.implication: A recurring expert-video library can make the concepts easier to understand before consultation.

observations.title: The team provides a natural rotating cast
observations.detail: The official roster includes ownership, marketing/interior design, lead design, production, project coordination, and site supervision.
observations.implication: A multi-expert format can demonstrate organizational depth and avoid over-reliance on one spokesperson.

scores.summary: Premium visual projects and unusually strong expert depth create excellent content fit; no strong 30-day buying-intent signal was verified.

concepts.title: Would This Survive Your Next Life Stage?
concepts.hook: Will this renovation still work when your life changes?
outline: Open on one floor-plan or circulation detail.
Show how it serves a homeowner's current life.
Show the living-in-place adaptation it supports later.
Close on one planning question to ask before renovation.
concepts.productionNotes: Use Lagois-approved Adaptiv examples and avoid medical or accessibility claims not supplied by the company.

concepts.title: Net Zero, One Detail at a Time
concepts.hook: This invisible detail changes how the whole room feels.
outline: Open on one envelope or efficiency detail during installation.
Have a site expert explain its purpose in plain language.
Show the finished surface that conceals it.
Close with one homeowner question.
concepts.productionNotes: Use verified project specifications. Do not make energy-savings claims without project data.

concepts.title: Sketch to Site
concepts.hook: This one pencil mark became the detail you notice first.
outline: Start with an approved sketch or plan mark.
Match cut to framing or rough-in.
Move to finish selection.
Reveal the completed space and the homeowner question that drove it.
concepts.productionNotes: Secure client consent and conceal plan details that identify an address.

outreach.subject: A Lagois expert series, plus one small LinkedIn fix
outreach.opening: Hi Emma, I was reviewing how Lagois explains net-zero renovation, Adaptiv living-in-place work, and the three-phase design-build process.
outreach.body: Those are strong differentiators, but they take several paragraphs to understand. Your team roster also gives you a natural cast for video: ownership, design, interiors, production, and site supervision. I mapped a recurring series called 'Net Zero, One Detail at a Time,' plus a 'Sketch to Site' format that connects a design decision to the built result. One small site note as well: the LinkedIn link in the current footer points to an admin dashboard path rather than the public company page. I'm with Que Media in Ottawa.
outreach.callToAction: If it is useful, I can send three complete shot outlines tailored to the services Lagois most wants to grow.

sources.label: Public company LinkedIn
sources.publisher: Lagois Design Build Renovate on LinkedIn

name: OakWood Design & Build
industry: Residential and commercial design-build, renovation, custom homes, and architectural design
location: Headquarters: 865 Taylor Creek Drive, Ottawa, ON K4A 0Z9; central studio: 555 Bank Street, Ottawa, ON K1S 5L7; trade partner location: 31 Northside Road, Ottawa, ON K2H 8S1
offerings: Project communication through the OakWood app
customerSegments: Homeowners seeking high-performance or technology-enabled construction

observations.title: Stray characters appear in the current homepage rendering
observations.detail: The current page crawl renders isolated N characters around proof points and feature blocks, including the service-since and project-count sections.
observations.implication: A template or icon-rendering check would improve polish on a premium service conversion page.

observations.title: A broad offer creates a long decision surface
observations.detail: Architectural design, homes, renovations, cabinetry, investment properties, commercial work, financing, sustainability, studios, and process all compete for attention on the main site.
observations.implication: Short video pathways can segment viewers by the specific decision they are making.

observations.title: The design studios are a distinctive content asset
observations.detail: OakWood says the Orleans design centre contains more than 7,500 products and displays and invites in-person tours.
observations.implication: A recurring showroom series can turn material and technology comparisons into local studio appointments.

scores.summary: Three local studios, a four-generation story, and deep visual inventory create exceptional content potential; the lack of a current trigger and likely incumbent sophistication lower response expectations.

concepts.title: Touch It Before You Build It
concepts.hook: These two materials look almost identical online. They do not feel or perform the same.
outline: Compare two materials in the Orleans design centre.
Show the tactile or performance difference up close.
Have a designer explain the homeowner decision each suits.
Close on an in-person studio visit.
concepts.productionNotes: Film under controlled showroom lighting with macro detail and label every product accurately. Avoid durability claims not supplied by the manufacturer.

concepts.title: The Budget Problem We Catch Before Construction
concepts.hook: This is where one design decision becomes a construction budget problem.
outline: Open on a concept or 3D model.
Show the architect, designer, and builder review sequence.
Explain the feasibility change before permit or demolition.
Close with one planning question for homeowners.
concepts.productionNotes: Use a de-identified approved case and avoid quoting costs without OakWood documentation.

concepts.title: Four Generations, One Detail
concepts.hook: This craft principle survived four generations, but the technology changed.
outline: Have John or Patricia select one legacy craft detail.
Show an archival or workshop reference if available.
Have a younger team member demonstrate the modern high-performance version.
Close on the principle that connects them.
concepts.productionNotes: Use family archive material only with OakWood approval. Keep the story specific to one detail per episode.

outreach.subject: A showroom-led video format for OakWood
outreach.opening: Hi Patricia, I was reviewing OakWood's current site and the physical design-centre offer stood out.
outreach.body: More than 7,500 products and displays gives you a strong local filming asset: homeowners can see why two materials that look similar online feel, age, or perform differently in person. I mapped a short series called 'Touch It Before You Build It,' filmed in the Orleans studio and closed on a design-centre visit. A second format would show one budget or feasibility problem the integrated architect, designer, and builder team catches before construction. One small web note: the current homepage crawl is also rendering isolated N characters between several proof points, which may be worth a template check. I'm with Que Media in Ottawa.
outreach.callToAction: I can send the first three scripts before asking for any time on your calendar.
